message:
feat(fcp-core): add canonical Display + as_str for EventSeverity

Why
---
Other variant enums in fcp-core (e.g., `OrderingPolicy` in this same
file, plus `ConnectorRoute`, `ConnectorLifecycleState`, etc.) follow
a consistent pattern: an inherent `as_str()` returning the canonical
wire/display string, plus a `Display` impl that delegates to it.
This gives one canonical formatting path for log lines, error
messages, and serde tag derivation, and lets future test pins assert
that `Display`, `as_str()`, and the JSON serde tag all agree.

`EventSeverity` was the odd one out — callers wanting a human form
had to either rely on `Debug` (which is not a stable contract) or
hand-roll a match. Bringing it in line with the rest of the module
removes that asymmetry and unblocks the next round of bead-driven
test pins for the event subsystem.

What
----
- `crates/fcp-core/src/event.rs`:
  - New inherent `EventSeverity::as_str(self) -> &'static str`
    returning the canonical lowercase tag for each variant:
    `Info => "info"`, `Notice => "notice"`,
    `Warning => "warning"`, `Error => "error"`,
    `Critical => "critical"`.
  - New `impl fmt::Display for EventSeverity` that writes
    `self.as_str()` directly via `f.write_str`.
  - `as_str` is `const`-callable so it can be used in `const`
    contexts (variant tables, lookup arrays).

Compatibility
-------------
Pure addition: no existing public types or signatures change.
The existing serde tag for `EventSeverity` is already lowercase
via the `#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]` attribute on the enum,
so the new `as_str` returns exactly the same strings the wire form
already uses. Callers that previously printed the variant via
`Debug` will see the new lowercase form when they migrate to
`Display`, but `Debug` itself is unchanged.

No new imports needed — `fmt` is already in scope via the existing
`use std::{cmp::Ordering as CmpOrdering, fmt};` line.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
